reasoning: The 10-year real interest rate typically follows a random walk, making short-term changes close to 50/50. However, the data reveals a moderate downward trend over the past year (dropping from ~2.05 to 1.55 prior to updating to 1.60 on Nov 1). Real rates have fallen somewhat as inflation dynamics shifted and the Fed maintains its policy stance, with an expectation it will continue easing or holding moderately downward pressure. The linear model predicts roughly a 30% chance of an increase based on recent slope, but actual rate movements have high variance. Thus, substituting some drift toward 50% for standard random walk behavior yields slightly below-even odds for the first date (~42%) and marginally closer to 50% for the later date (~46%) as uncertainty increases with the extended time horizon.
batch_queries: Will the Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland's estimate for the 10-year real interest rate have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-11-09?
